title: Model the build as targets, not directories
description: Express the build as libraries and executables with properties attached per target, instead of directory-scoped commands that leak settings everywhere.
body: Use when authoring or modernizing a CMakeLists that still relies on include_directories or add_definitions at directory scope. Do not use global commands that apply flags to every target in a subtree. Actions: define each component with add_library or add_executable; attach includes, definitions, and options with the target_* commands; let consumers inherit requirements by linking. Validation signals include a consumer that compiles using only target_link_libraries, with no restated include paths. Risk boundary: directory-wide flags create hidden coupling that breaks when files move, so prefer per-target properties from the start.
